javascript.title: JavaScript Core
javascript.lede: JavaScript single-threaded কিন্তু non-blocking। Event loop-এর জন্য async code পারে। Closure, prototype, this - এই তিনটা বুঝলে বাকি সব interpret করতে পারবেন।
javascript.content:
Closure

Inner function outer function-এর variable remember করে, even after outer function return করে।

function counter() {
  let count = 0;
  return () => ++count;
}
const c = counter();
c(); // 1
c(); // 2

Prototype chain

প্রতিটা object-এর একটা __proto__ থাকে। Property lookup fail করলে prototype chain-এ উপরে খোঁজে, শেষে Object.prototype, তারপর null।

Event Loop - deeper view

┌──────────────────────────┐
│  Call Stack              │  ← synchronous code
├──────────────────────────┤
│  Microtask Queue         │  ← Promise.then, queueMicrotask, MutationObserver
├──────────────────────────┤
│  Macrotask Queue         │  ← setTimeout, setInterval, I/O, UI event
├──────────────────────────┤
│  Render Steps (browser)  │  ← rAF → style → layout → paint
└──────────────────────────┘

Stack empty হলে event loop সব microtask drain করে (nested microtask-সহ), তারপর একটা macrotask নেয়, তারপর browser render step। Recursive microtask দিলে UI starve হবে।

Node.js event loop phases

timers → pending callbacks → idle/prepare
     → poll (I/O) → check (setImmediate) → close callbacks
     (প্রতি phase-এর পর microtask + process.nextTick drain)

this কীভাবে bind হয়?

  1. new দিয়ে call → newly created object
  2. obj.method() → obj
  3. call/apply/bind → explicit
  4. arrow function → lexically inherited
  5. কিছুই না → global (strict-এ undefined)

Iterators & Generators

// Custom iterable
const range = {
  [Symbol.iterator]() {
    let i = 0;
    return { next: () => i < 3 ? { value: i++, done: false } : { value: undefined, done: true } };
  }
};
for (const x of range) console.log(x); // 0,1,2

// Generator - simpler
function* gen() { yield 1; yield 2; }

Async iteration

async function* pages() {
  let cursor = null;
  do {
    const r = await fetch(`/api?cursor=${cursor}`).then(r => r.json());
    cursor = r.nextCursor;
    yield* r.items;
  } while (cursor);
}

for await (const item of pages()) console.log(item);

Modules - ESM vs CJS quick diff

AspectESMCJS
LoadStatic, asyncDynamic, sync
Syntaximport/exportrequire/module.exports
Tree-shaking✓✗
Top-level await✓✗
Circular depsLive binding, live valuePartial export

WeakRef & FinalizationRegistry

Advanced memory management। WeakRef-এ object hold করে কিন্তু GC block করে না। FinalizationRegistry.register(obj, meta)-এ object collect হলে callback fire - cache eviction, resource cleanup।

Structured cloning

const clone = structuredClone(obj);
// Deep clone - Date, Map, Set, RegExp, ArrayBuffer, circular ref সব handle

Common pitfalls

  • typeof null === 'object' - historical bug, use x === null
  • NaN === NaN - false! use Number.isNaN()
  • 0.1 + 0.2 !== 0.3 - IEEE 754 float; use toFixed বা Decimal library
  • Array-এ sparse hole - [1,,3].map(x => x) hole skip করে
  • {a: 1} === {a: 1} - false, reference compare

Garbage Collection basics (V8)

  • Young generation: scavenger (Cheney's algorithm) - short-lived object, fast
  • Old generation: mark-sweep-compact - long-lived
  • Incremental + concurrent - GC pause কমায়
  • Memory leak source: closures holding DOM, global arrays, event listener not removed

typescript.title: TypeScript
typescript.lede: TypeScript শুধু "JS + type annotation" না - এটা একটা পুরো type-level programming language। Generics, conditional types, mapped types দিয়ে compile-time-এ logic execute করা যায়।
typescript.content:
Basic Generics

function identity<T>(value: T): T {
  return value;
}
identity<string>('hi'); // T = string
identity(42);        // T inferred as number

Generic constraint

function longest<T extends { length: number }>(a: T, b: T): T {
  return a.length >= b.length ? a : b;
}
longest('foo', 'hello');  // ✓
longest([1,2], [3,4,5]);   // ✓
longest(5, 10);            // ✗ number-এ length নেই

keyof, typeof, indexed access

type User = { id: number; name: string };
type K = keyof User;          // 'id' | 'name'
type Name = User['name'];      // string

const u = { id: 1, name: 'A' };
type U = typeof u;             // { id: number; name: string }

Mapped types

type ReadonlyAll<T> = {
  readonly [K in keyof T]: T[K];
};
type Nullable<T> = { [K in keyof T]: T[K] | null };

Conditional types + infer

type IsString<T> = T extends string ? true : false;
type A = IsString<'hi'>;    // true
type B = IsString<42>;      // false

// infer দিয়ে return type extract
type ReturnT<F> = F extends (...a: any[]) => infer R ? R : never;
type R = ReturnT<() => number>;  // number

Utility types (built-in)

Utilityকাজ
Partial<T>সব property optional
Required<T>সব required
Readonly<T>সব readonly
Pick<T, K>K subset নেয়
Omit<T, K>K বাদ দেয়
Record<K, V>Key-value map type
ReturnType<F>Function-এর return
Parameters<F>Function args tuple
Awaited<P>Promise unwrap

Discriminated union

type Shape =
  | { kind: 'circle'; radius: number }
  | { kind: 'square'; side: number };

function area(s: Shape): number {
  switch (s.kind) {
    case 'circle': return Math.PI * s.radius ** 2;
    case 'square': return s.side ** 2;
  }
}

never, unknown, any

  • any - type check bypass, avoid।
  • unknown - type-safe any, narrow করে ব্যবহার।
  • never - impossible value (exhaustive check, error throw)।

as const & satisfies

const config = { mode: 'dark' } as const;
// config.mode: 'dark' (literal), not string

const colors = { red: '#f00' } satisfies Record<string, string>;
// type checked against constraint, কিন্তু literal type preserve

Template literal types

type Event = 'click' | 'hover';
type Handler = `on${Capitalize<Event>}`;
// 'onClick' | 'onHover'

Variance (covariance, contravariance, bivariance)

type Animal = { name: string };
type Dog = Animal & { bark: () => void };

// Array covariant - Dog[] ≤ Animal[]
const dogs: Dog[] = [];
const animals: Animal[] = dogs; // ✓

// Function parameter contravariant - (x: Animal) => void ≤ (x: Dog) => void
type HandleAnimal = (a: Animal) => void;
type HandleDog = (d: Dog) => void;
const h: HandleDog = (d: Animal) => {}; // ✓ strictFunctionTypes

Branded / Nominal types

type Brand<T, B> = T & { __brand: B };
type UserId = Brand<string, 'UserId'>;
type OrderId = Brand<string, 'OrderId'>;

function toUserId(s: string): UserId { return s as UserId; }
const u: UserId = toUserId('abc');
const o: OrderId = u; // ✗ error! type mismatch

Structural typing-এ string-এ mix up avoid। ID, currency, sanitized HTML-এ widely used।

Recursive types

type JSONValue =
  | string | number | boolean | null
  | JSONValue[]
  | { [key: string]: JSONValue };

// Recursive depth limit (~50) - DeepReadonly
type DeepReadonly<T> = {
  readonly [K in keyof T]: T[K] extends object ? DeepReadonly<T[K]> : T[K];
};

Module augmentation

// Extend third-party module
declare module 'express' {
  interface Request {
    user?: { id: string; role: string };
  }
}

// Extend global
declare global {
  interface Window { __APP__: { version: string }; }
}

Declaration files (.d.ts)

  • Library type definitions - no runtime code
  • Ambient: declare module 'pkg' (CommonJS legacy typings)
  • Triple-slash: /// <reference types="node" />
  • DefinitelyTyped (@types/*) community-maintained

tsconfig strictness - team-recommended

{
  "compilerOptions": {
    "strict": true,
    "noUncheckedIndexedAccess": true,  // arr[i] → T | undefined
    "exactOptionalPropertyTypes": true, // ?: T !== T | undefined
    "noImplicitOverride": true,
    "noPropertyAccessFromIndexSignature": true,
    "verbatimModuleSyntax": true
  }
}

Advanced patterns

  • Discriminated union exhaustive - assertNever(x: never) switch default-এ
  • Builder type - fluent API সব field set হলে tagged Complete type
  • Type predicate - function isUser(x: unknown): x is User
  • Assertion function - function assert(cond: boolean): asserts cond
  • const generics (TS 5+) - function f<const T>(x: T) literal preserve
Type-level trick: T extends U ? X : Y distribute হয় union-এর উপর। IsString<'a' | 42> = true | false = boolean। [T] extends [U] দিয়ে distribution disable করা যায়।
Zero-runtime philosophy: TS compile time-এ erase হয়। Runtime validation-এ Zod/Valibot - same schema থেকে z.infer দিয়ে TS type auto - single source of truth।

react.title: React Fundamentals
react.lede: React = UI library। আপনি JSX দিয়ে "UI কী দেখাবে" describe করেন, React "কীভাবে update হবে" handle করে। Core ধারণা: component, props, state, hook।
react.content:
Component-এর তিন ধরন

Typeকখন
Functional + hooksআধুনিক standard
Class componentLegacy, error boundary এখনও class
Server ComponentReact 19+, server-এ render

Props vs State

  • Props - parent থেকে পাওয়া immutable data।
  • State - component-এর নিজের mutable data। Change হলে re-render।

Hook-এর rules

  1. শুধু top-level-এ call করুন - loop/condition/nested function-এ না।
  2. শুধু React function (component বা custom hook)-এ।

গুরুত্বপূর্ণ hook

useState(initial)          // local state
useEffect(fn, deps)        // side effect
useMemo(fn, deps)          // memoize value
useCallback(fn, deps)      // memoize function
useRef(initial)            // mutable ref, no re-render
useContext(ctx)            // consume context
useReducer(reducer, init)  // complex state
useTransition()            // mark as low-priority
useDeferredValue(value)    // defer expensive update

Lifecycle mapping (class → hook)

  • componentDidMount → useEffect(fn, [])
  • componentDidUpdate → useEffect(fn, [dep])
  • componentWillUnmount → useEffect cleanup return

Key prop কেন দরকার?

List render করলে React index-based compare করে default-এ। যদি item reorder/insert/delete হয়, React ভুল DOM update করতে পারে। Stable unique key (database id) দিলে React accurately reconcile করে।

সতর্কতা: Array index-কে key হিসেবে ব্যবহার করবেন না যদি list dynamically mutate হয়। Static list হলে ঠিক আছে।

React 19 - কী নতুন?

Featureকাজ
use(promise)Promise suspend, conditionally callable (hook rule exception!)
useActionStateForm action-এর state/error/pending manage
useFormStatusParent form-এর pending status child-এ
useOptimisticServer confirm-এর আগে optimistic UI
Server Actions'use server' async fn, client form action হিসেবে call
Asset loadingCSS/font/script suspense-integrated
ref as propforwardRef-এর প্রয়োজন নেই
React CompilerAuto memoization compile time

Server Components (RSC)

Server-এ render হয়, JS client-এ ship হয় না। Direct DB/file access OK।

  • No state (useState), no effect (useEffect)
  • Async component allowed - async function Page() { ... }
  • Client boundary - 'use client' directive-এ
  • Server component serializable prop (JSON + function ref) client-এ pass করতে পারে

Concurrent rendering in depth

  • Fiber: reconciliation unit, interruptible
  • Scheduler: time-slice in 5ms, browser main thread-এ yield
  • Priority lanes: SyncLane, InputContinuousLane, DefaultLane, TransitionLane, IdleLane
  • Batching: 18+ automatic - promise, timeout, native event সব
  • Tearing prevention: useSyncExternalStore snapshot-based

Suspense patterns

// Code-split
const Chart = lazy(() => import('./Chart'));
<Suspense fallback={<Skeleton />}><Chart /></Suspense>

// Data - React 19 + framework
async function Posts() {
  const posts = await db.posts.findMany();
  return posts.map(p => <Post key={p.id} {...p} />);
}
<Suspense fallback={<Spinner />}><Posts /></Suspense>

Error Boundary gotchas

  • Render/lifecycle error catch করে - event handler, async, SSR catch করে না
  • এখনো class-only - React 19-এ react-error-boundary lib popular
  • Multiple boundary nest করে granular fallback possible

Performance checklist

  • React DevTools Profiler-এ "why did this render?"
  • Props stable রাখুন - useMemo/useCallback
  • Context split - high-frequency change আলাদা context-এ
  • State colocate - top-level-এ রাখবেন না যদি দরকার না হয়
  • Large list virtualize (react-window, @tanstack/react-virtual)
  • Expensive derived state useMemo
  • React Compiler auto-memoize (opt-in 2026-এ widespread)

reactivity.title: Reactivity Models
reactivity.lede: "State change হলে UI কীভাবে update হবে?" - এই প্রশ্নের উত্তরে frameworks আলাদা strategy নেয়। VDOM, signals, observables - কে কোনটা কখন use করে দেখা যাক।
reactivity.content:
তিনটা পদ্ধতি

Approachকীভাবে কাজ করেFramework
Virtual DOM diffingState change → পুরো subtree re-render → old vs new VDOM diff → minimal DOM updateReact, Preact
Fine-grained signalsপ্রতিটা value-কে reactive atom বানায়, শুধু যে DOM node depend করে সেটাই updateSolidJS, Svelte 5, Vue 3 reactivity, Signals proposal
Observable streamsStream-ভিত্তিক, subscribe-এ update। Reactive programming।RxJS, Angular
Dirty checkingDigest cycle-এ প্রতি scope checkAngularJS (legacy)

React VDOM - কী আর কেন

state update
   ↓
function re-run (সম্পূর্ণ tree)
   ↓
new VDOM tree
   ↓
Diff with previous VDOM
   ↓
Minimal patches to real DOM

সুবিধা: developer ergonomic, প্রতি render fresh code। অসুবিধা: unrelated re-render খরচ, তাই memoization লাগে।

Signals - SolidJS-এর example

const [count, setCount] = createSignal(0);
// component function চলে মাত্র একবার
return <div>{count()}</div>;
// setCount(1) শুধু ওই text node update করে

সুবিধা: by default fast, memoization almost automatic। অসুবিধা: React-এর মতো "just call a function" mental model ভেঙে যায়।

React-এও FGR সম্ভব (useSyncExternalStore)

Fine-grained reactivity শুধু SolidJS/Svelte-এর privilege না। React-এর official API useSyncExternalStore দিয়ে external signal store-এ subscribe করে same granularity-তে update করা যায় - Concurrent Mode/StrictMode/SSR-এর সাথে fully compatible।

// @preact/signals-react
import { signal } from '@preact/signals-react';
const count = signal(0);
function Counter() {
  return <div>{count}</div>;
  // শুধু ওই text node update হয়, component re-run হয় না
}

Signal library (@preact/signals-react, @legendapp/state, @tanstack/store, mobx-react-lite, Jotai, Zustand) internally useSyncExternalStore use করে। Tearing-safe, concurrent-safe, fine-grained।

React-এ re-render কখন হয়?

  • State change (setState, useReducer dispatch)
  • Parent re-render (child props-এ change না হলেও)
  • Context value change (সব consumer)
  • Subscribing hook-এ update (useSyncExternalStore)

Re-render কমানোর উপায়

  • React.memo - props-এ shallow compare
  • useMemo - expensive computation cache
  • useCallback - function reference stable
  • State-কে colocate - যেখানে দরকার, শুধু সেখানে
  • React 19-এর compiler auto memoization

TC39 Signals Proposal

Language-level signals standardize করার proposal (Stage 1, 2024+)। Target: framework-agnostic reactive primitive, সব framework এটার উপর build করতে পারবে।

const counter = new Signal.State(0);
const doubled = new Signal.Computed(() => counter.get() * 2);

counter.set(5);
doubled.get(); // 10

Tearing - কী এবং কেন

Concurrent rendering-এ React একই render-এর মাঝে pause করে অন্য urgent কাজ করতে পারে। Pause-এর সময় external store mutate হলে, render-এর first half এ old value, second half-এ new - output inconsistent (tear)।

// Unsafe - plain external subscription
let count = 0;
function Component() {
  // Render 1 সময় count=0, pause, mutate to 5, Render 2 সময় count=5 - tear!
}

// Safe - useSyncExternalStore
const count = useSyncExternalStore(
  subscribe,
  () => store.get(),       // client snapshot
  () => store.getServer()  // SSR snapshot
);

MobX reactivity patterns

class Store {
  @observable count = 0;
  @computed get doubled() { return this.count * 2; }
  @action increment() { this.count++; }
}
// component-এ observer(() => ...) auto-subscribe

Proxy vs getter/setter tracking

ApproachProsCons
Object.defineProperty (Vue 2, MobX legacy)Wide browser supportArray index, new property detect করতে পারে না
Proxy (Vue 3, Valtio)সব mutation intercept - nested, array, delete, new propES2015+ only (no IE)
Signal (explicit .value)Fast, no proxy overheadVerbose syntax

Effect scheduling

  • Eager: sync-এ signal update-এ dependent effect immediate run
  • Batched: microtask-এর শেষে (Vue, modern SolidJS) - burst update একবারে
  • Scheduled: rAF-aligned (UI update smooth)
  • React unstable_batchedUpdates/automatic batching 18-এ

Immutability - structural sharing

Large tree update-এ whole tree copy inefficient। Immer: draft proxy দিয়ে mutate-like syntax, behind-the-scene shallow copy + reference reuse।

import produce from 'immer';
const next = produce(state, draft => {
  draft.users[0].name = 'new';  // only changed path copied
});

Reactivity interop - 2026 landscape

  • Preact Signals - @preact/signals-react React-এ
  • TanStack Store - framework-agnostic FGR
  • Legend State - ultra-fast signals + persistence
  • Jotai/Zustand - atom/store-based, widely adopted
  • Valtio - proxy-based, minimal API
  • MobX - observable class-first (OOP-friendly)

performance.title: Performance ও Virtualization
performance.lede: 10,000 rows-এর table browser-এ render করলে scroll laggy, memory ফুল। সমাধান: শুধু viewport-এর row DOM-এ রাখুন। এটাই virtualization।
performance.content:
Virtualization কীভাবে কাজ করে

Total rows: 10,000
Visible rows (viewport): 20
Rendered in DOM: 20 + buffer (say 5) = 25

Scroll হলে:
  - কোন row visible সেটা calculate
  - DOM node recycle/recreate
  - Container-এ total height আগেই spacer হিসেবে দেয়া

Popular virtualization libraries

Libraryবিশেষত্ব
react-windowLight, simple, fixed বা variable size।
@tanstack/react-virtualFramework-agnostic, hook-based, flexible।
react-virtualizedপুরনো, feature-rich কিন্তু বড়।
AG Grid / MUI DataGridProduction-grade, built-in virtualization।

Memoization principles

  • useMemo(fn, deps) - deps change না হলে same value return।
  • useCallback(fn, deps) - useMemo-র function version।
  • React.memo(Component) - props shallow equal হলে skip।
Rule: Premature memoization useless। প্রথমে React DevTools-এ profile করুন, actual slowness পেলে memoize।

Code splitting

// Route-based
const Admin = lazy(() => import('./Admin'));
<Suspense fallback={<Loader />}><Admin /></Suspense>

Debounce vs Throttle

Techniqueকাজকখন
DebounceEvent থামার পর একবার callSearch input, resize
Throttleপ্রতি N ms-এ max একবারScroll, mousemove

Layout thrashing

DOM read (offsetHeight, getBoundingClientRect) আর write (style change) interleave করলে browser বারবার reflow করে। Batch করুন: সব read আগে, সব write পরে।

Core Web Vitals (2026 standard)

MetricGoodOptimize by
LCP (Largest Contentful Paint)≤ 2.5sHero image preload, CDN, TTFB ↓, inline critical CSS
INP (Interaction to Next Paint, replaced FID 2024)≤ 200msLong task break, useTransition, debounce, worker offload
CLS (Cumulative Layout Shift)≤ 0.1Image width/height, font-display swap carefully, reserve ad slots

Image optimization deep

  • Format: AVIF (best compression) → WebP fallback → JPEG/PNG
  • Responsive: <img srcset="400w.jpg 400w, 800w.jpg 800w" sizes="(max-width: 600px) 100vw, 50vw">
  • Lazy: loading="lazy" native, fetchpriority="high" for LCP hero
  • Placeholder: blurhash/thumbhash low-res inline
  • Modern loaders: Next.js Image, Cloudflare Images, imgproxy

Resource hints

<!-- DNS + TCP early -->
<link rel="preconnect" href="https://api.example.com">
<link rel="dns-prefetch" href="https://cdn.example.com">

<!-- Fetch high priority -->
<link rel="preload" as="image" href="hero.avif" fetchpriority="high">
<link rel="preload" as="font" type="font/woff2" href="f.woff2" crossorigin>

<!-- Future navigation -->
<link rel="prefetch" href="/dashboard" as="document">
<script type="speculationrules">
{"prerender": [{"urls": ["/dashboard"]}]}
</script>

JS execution budget

  • Mobile 3G: 170KB gzipped JS upper bound (old rule, INP-friendly)
  • Long task (>50ms) block main thread - split or offload
  • scheduler.yield() (Chrome 2024+) - cooperative scheduling
  • Heavy parse → lazy chunk import
  • CSS-in-JS runtime cost - prefer compile-time (Tailwind, vanilla-extract, Panda CSS)

Rendering performance metrics

MetricMeasure
TTFBServer + network roundtrip
FCPFirst paint (anything on screen)
LCPLargest above-fold content
TTITime to Interactive
TBTTotal Blocking Time (lab)
INPReal-world interaction latency (field)

Tools

  • Lab: Lighthouse, WebPageTest, Chrome DevTools Performance panel
  • Field (RUM): Google Search Console CrUX, Sentry Performance, DataDog RUM, Cloudflare Web Analytics
  • Bundle analysis: webpack-bundle-analyzer, rollup-plugin-visualizer, vite build --analyze
  • Profiler: React DevTools Profiler, Chrome Performance panel

pagination.title: Pagination - Offset vs Cursor
pagination.lede: 10M row-এর table-এ "Page 100,000" দেখাতে চাইলে offset-based SQL slow। Cursor-based pagination সেই সমস্যার solution।
pagination.content:
Offset-based

SELECT * FROM posts
ORDER BY created_at DESC
LIMIT 20 OFFSET 100000;

সুবিধা: "Page 5 of 100" দেখানো সহজ, jump করা যায়।
অসুবিধা: DB-কে 100,020টা row scan করতে হয়, page যত গভীর, query তত slow। Insert হলে rows shift - duplicate/miss হতে পারে।

Cursor-based (keyset)

-- প্রথম request
SELECT * FROM posts ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 20;

-- পরের page: last id = 981 (cursor)
SELECT * FROM posts
WHERE id < 981
ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 20;

সুবিধা: indexed lookup, constant time। Real-time data-এ stable। Duplicate নেই।
অসুবিধা: random page jump নেই, "Page 5" directly যাওয়া যায় না।

Infinite Scroll vs Load More vs Page numbers

Infinite Scroll

Social feed-এ ভালো। Accessibility issue, footer unreachable। IntersectionObserver দিয়ে implement।

Load More button

Best of both - user control পায়, accessibility ভালো।

Page numbers

Traditional, SEO-friendly, bookmark করা যায়। Deep page slow (offset)।

API design

// Cursor-based API
GET /posts?limit=20&cursor=eyJpZCI6OTgxfQ==

{
  "data": [...],
  "nextCursor": "eyJpZCI6OTYxfQ==",
  "hasMore": true
}

Cursor-টা opaque base64 দিয়ে encode করুন - client যেন internal logic-এ নির্ভর না করে।

Keyset pagination + sort

Multi-column sort-এ cursor হবে tuple: (created_at, id)। Tie-breaker দিতে index-এর সাথে composite key দরকার।

-- created_at DESC, id DESC দিয়ে sort
SELECT * FROM posts
WHERE (created_at, id) < ($cursor_created_at, $cursor_id)
ORDER BY created_at DESC, id DESC
LIMIT 20;

Cursor encoding - best practices

  • Opaque: base64 encode internal JSON - client নির্ভর করবে না structure-এ
  • Signed: HMAC দিয়ে tamper-detect (user cursor forge করে অন্য data access-এর চেষ্টা করলে ধরা পড়বে)
  • Versioned: v1:... prefix - API evolve করলে forward-compatible
  • Stable: column value rename/type change → invalidate
  • Short TTL optional - stale cursor reject, force refresh

GraphQL Relay-style connections

query {
  posts(first: 20, after: "cursor123") {
    edges { node { id title }, cursor }
    pageInfo { hasNextPage, endCursor }
  }
}

Standardized shape - pagination client library (Apollo, urql) auto-handle। Bi-directional: first/after + last/before।

Infinite scroll UX pitfalls

  • Footer unreachable - sticky footer বা "load more" button hybrid
  • Scroll position lost on back navigation - restore strategy (History API state)
  • Accessibility - keyboard users stuck; provide "show more" button + aria-live
  • SEO - crawler paginated content miss করে; server-render initial pages
  • Memory - old items DOM-এ accumulate; virtualize + unmount offscreen

Real-time pagination challenges

Feed-এ while scrolling নতুন post-ও আসছে। Offset-এ duplicate/missed item, cursor-এ stable।

  • "New posts above" banner (Twitter-style) - user control পায়
  • Optimistic insert at top, don't shift existing cursor
  • Websocket/SSE live update - new items buffer, user click to reveal

Keyset + search/filter combo

Filter (WHERE user_id = 42) + sort (ORDER BY created_at DESC)-এ cursor তৈরিতে সেই ordering column টাই নিতে হবে। Full-text search-এ ranking score tie-break করতে id add।

SELECT * FROM posts
WHERE user_id = 42
  AND (created_at, id) < ($c_time, $c_id)
ORDER BY created_at DESC, id DESC
LIMIT 20;

-- Supporting index
CREATE INDEX ON posts (user_id, created_at DESC, id DESC);

http.title: HTTP ও Basic Server
http.lede: Every web app HTTP-এর উপর চলে। Request, response, method, status code, header - এই basics solid হলে REST API, Express, authentication সব সহজ।
http.content:
HTTP methods

MethodকাজIdempotent?Safe?
GETRetrieve✓✓
HEADHeaders only✓✓
POSTCreate/process✗✗
PUTReplace✓✗
PATCHPartial update✗ (usually)✗
DELETERemove✓✗
OPTIONSAllowed methods✓✓

Idempotent = একবার বা 100 বার call করলে result same।

Status code ranges

  • 1xx - Informational
  • 2xx - Success (200 OK, 201 Created, 204 No Content)
  • 3xx - Redirect (301 permanent, 302 temporary, 304 Not Modified)
  • 4xx - Client error (400 Bad Request, 401 Unauth, 403 Forbidden, 404, 409 Conflict, 422 Unprocessable, 429 Too Many)
  • 5xx - Server error (500, 502 Bad Gateway, 503 Service Unavailable, 504 Timeout)

Simplest HTTP server (Node)

import { createServer } from 'node:http';

createServer((req, res) => {
  res.writeHead(200, { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' });
  res.end(JSON.stringify({ ok: true }));
}).listen(3000);

Bun.serve - modern replacement

Bun.serve({
  port: 3000,
  routes: {
    '/api/users/:id': {
      GET: req => Response.json({ id: req.params.id })
    }
  }
});

CORS in short

Browser same-origin policy enforce করে। Different origin থেকে request-এ server-কে Access-Control-Allow-Origin header দিতে হয়। Non-simple request-এ browser আগে OPTIONS preflight পাঠায়।

REST constraints (Fielding)

  • Client-server separation
  • Stateless
  • Cacheable
  • Uniform interface
  • Layered system
  • Code on demand (optional)

HTTP/1.1 → HTTP/2 → HTTP/3

VersionTransportKey feature
HTTP/1.1TCPKeep-alive, text protocol, head-of-line blocking
HTTP/2TCP + TLSBinary framing, multiplex streams, header compression (HPACK), server push (deprecated)
HTTP/3QUIC (UDP)No TCP HoL blocking, connection migration, 0-RTT resume, faster on lossy mobile

Content negotiation

Client:
Accept: application/json;q=0.9, text/html;q=0.5
Accept-Language: bn, en;q=0.8
Accept-Encoding: gzip, br, zstd

Server picks best match + returns:
Content-Type: application/json
Content-Language: bn
Content-Encoding: br
Vary: Accept, Accept-Language, Accept-Encoding

Conditional requests - caching + bandwidth

# First request
GET /avatar.jpg
→ 200 OK, ETag: "abc123", Last-Modified: ...

# Next time - client sends hint
GET /avatar.jpg
If-None-Match: "abc123"
→ 304 Not Modified (no body, tiny response)

Streaming responses

  • Chunked transfer encoding - Content-Length unknown, chunks পাঠানো
  • Server-Sent Events (SSE) - text stream, auto-reconnect, one-way server→client
  • WebSocket - full-duplex, binary + text, upgrade from HTTP
  • HTTP/2 stream - response body chunk as frames
  • Long polling (legacy) - server hold request until event

SSE vs WebSocket - choose

NeedChoice
Server notifications onlySSE (simpler, HTTP-friendly, auto-reconnect)
Chat, collaborative editingWebSocket (bidirectional)
Binary data (game, audio)WebSocket (or WebRTC)
AI streaming (LLM token)SSE (OpenAI, Anthropic use)

Cookie attributes deep

  • HttpOnly - JS access block (XSS mitigation)
  • Secure - HTTPS only
  • SameSite=Strict/Lax/None - CSRF mitigation (Lax default browser)
  • Partitioned - CHIPS (Cookies Having Independent Partitioned State) - third-party cookie privacy
  • Max-Age vs Expires - former simpler, preferred

Authentication mechanisms compared

TypeUse case
Session cookieTraditional web app, same-origin
JWT (Bearer)Stateless API, mobile, cross-service
OAuth 2.0 + PKCEThird-party login, SPA
OpenID ConnectOAuth + identity (SSO)
mTLSService-to-service auth (zero-trust)
Passkeys / WebAuthnPassword-less, phishing-resistant

Error response patterns

Problem Details (RFC 7807) - consistent JSON error body:

HTTP/1.1 422 Unprocessable Entity
Content-Type: application/problem+json

{
  "type": "https://example.com/errors/validation",
  "title": "Validation failed",
  "status": 422,
  "detail": "email must be valid",
  "instance": "/users/create",
  "errors": [{ "field": "email", "code": "invalid_format" }]
}

express.title: Express ও OOP API design
express.lede: Express = minimalist Node.js HTTP framework। Middleware chain-ভিত্তিক। OOP + SOLID apply করলে controller/service/repository-তে organized architecture বানানো যায়।
express.content:
Request lifecycle

Request →
  middleware 1 → next()
  middleware 2 → next()
  route handler → res.send()
        ↓
  error middleware (err থাকলে)
        ↓
  Response

Hello-world Express

import express from 'express';
const app = express();

app.use(express.json());            // body parser
app.get('/health', (req, res) => res.json({ ok: true }));
app.post('/users', (req, res) => {
  const user = createUser(req.body);
  res.status(201).json(user);
});

// error middleware (4 args)
app.use((err, req, res, next) => {
  res.status(500).json({ error: err.message });
});

app.listen(3000);

Middleware কী?

(req, res, next) => { ... } signature-এর function। Request চলাকালীন ডাকা হয়, next() call করলে পরেরটা।

  • Application-level: app.use(...)
  • Router-level: router.use(...)
  • Route-level: handler-এর আগে
  • Error-handling: 4 args (err, req, res, next)
  • Built-in: express.json(), express.static()
  • Third-party: helmet, cors, morgan, rate-limit

Organized structure (OOP + SOLID)

src/
├── routes/       // HTTP routing only
├── controllers/  // Request/response handling
├── services/     // Business logic
├── repositories/ // DB access layer
├── models/       // Domain entities
├── middleware/   // Cross-cutting concerns
└── utils/

Class-based controller example

class UserController {
  constructor(private userService: UserService) {}

  async create(req, res, next) {
    try {
      const user = await this.userService.create(req.body);
      res.status(201).json(user);
    } catch (e) { next(e); }
  }
}
Modern alternatives: Fastify (faster), NestJS (full-blown OOP framework), Hono (edge-friendly), Bun.serve (native)।

Express 5 - কী পরিবর্তন

  • Promise support - async route-এ error auto next(err)-এ propagate, express-async-errors-এর দরকার নেই
  • Deprecated API removed (app.del → app.delete)
  • Regex path cautious default (ReDoS mitigation)
  • Node 18+ minimum
  • Still minimalist - Fastify/Hono-এর faster, কিন্তু ecosystem Express-এ biggest

Validation layer - Zod + middleware

import { z } from 'zod';

const CreateUserSchema = z.object({
  email: z.string().email(),
  age: z.number().int().min(18)
});

const validate = (schema) => (req, res, next) => {
  const r = schema.safeParse(req.body);
  if (!r.success) {
    return res.status(422).json({ errors: r.error.flatten() });
  }
  req.body = r.data;
  next();
};

app.post('/users', validate(CreateUserSchema), controller.create);

Observability hooks

// Request context - correlation id
app.use((req, res, next) => {
  req.id = req.headers['x-request-id'] ?? crypto.randomUUID();
  res.setHeader('x-request-id', req.id);
  next();
});

// Structured log (pino)
app.use(pinoHttp({ logger, customProps: (req) => ({ reqId: req.id }) }));

// Metric (prom-client)
app.use(metricsMiddleware);

// Trace (OpenTelemetry) - auto-instrumentation-এ setup

Error taxonomy

class AppError extends Error {
  constructor(public status: number, public code: string, msg: string) {
    super(msg);
  }
}
class NotFoundError extends AppError {
  constructor(resource: string) { super(404, 'not_found', `${resource} not found`); }
}
class ValidationError extends AppError { ... }
class AuthError extends AppError { ... }

// Error middleware central
app.use((err, req, res, next) => {
  if (err instanceof AppError) {
    return res.status(err.status).json({ code: err.code, message: err.message });
  }
  logger.error({ err, reqId: req.id });
  res.status(500).json({ code: 'internal_error' });
});

Graceful shutdown

const server = app.listen(3000);

function shutdown() {
  logger.info('shutting down');
  server.close(async () => {
    await db.end();
    await queue.close();
    process.exit(0);
  });
  setTimeout(() => process.exit(1), 30_000); // hard kill fallback
}

process.on('SIGTERM', shutdown);
process.on('SIGINT', shutdown);

OpenAPI - spec first vs code first

  • Spec first: YAML/JSON OpenAPI schema → code generator দিয়ে server stub + client SDK। Contract-first team-এ ভালো।
  • Code first: Zod/TypeBox schema → zod-to-openapi দিয়ে spec generate। Developer-friendly।
  • Full-type RPC: tRPC - no OpenAPI, TS types directly share। Same-monorepo context-এ powerful।

Testing strategy

  • Unit: service/utility pure function - vitest/bun test
  • Integration: controller + DB (testcontainers or sqlite :memory:) - supertest
  • Contract: OpenAPI schema-এর বিপরীতে validate
  • E2E: Playwright/Cypress - full app + DB + queue

oop.title: OOP
oop.lede: Encapsulation, Inheritance, Polymorphism, Abstraction - এই চারটা concept দিয়ে real-world জিনিসকে code-এ model করা হয়।
oop.content:
চার পিলার সংক্ষেপে

Pillarকীউদাহরণ
EncapsulationData + behavior-কে এক unit-এ bind, internal detail hideclass-এর private field
Inheritanceএক class-এর member আরেকটা class পায়class Dog extends Animal
Polymorphismএক interface, বিভিন্ন behaviorMethod override, duck typing
AbstractionEssential feature দেখানো, complexity hideInterface, abstract class

Class vs Object vs Instance

  • Class - blueprint (যেমন class Car)
  • Object / Instance - blueprint থেকে বানানো actual জিনিস (new Car())

TypeScript-এ পুরো example

abstract class Animal {
  constructor(protected name: string) {}
  abstract sound(): string;
  describe(): string {
    return `${this.name} says ${this.sound()}`;
  }
}

class Dog extends Animal {
  sound() { return 'Woof'; }
}

class Cat extends Animal {
  sound() { return 'Meow'; }
}

const animals: Animal[] = [new Dog('Rex'), new Cat('Kitty')];
animals.forEach(a => console.log(a.describe())); // polymorphism

Composition > Inheritance

"Prefer composition over inheritance" - GoF classic। Inheritance tight coupling তৈরি করে, deep hierarchy fragile। Composition (has-a) flexible।

// Inheritance (tight)
class ElectricCar extends Car { ... }

// Composition (flexible)
class Car {
  constructor(private engine: Engine) {}
}
new Car(new ElectricEngine());
new Car(new GasEngine());

Access modifier

  • public - সবাই access করতে পারে (default)
  • protected - class + subclass
  • private - শুধু ওই class-এর ভেতর

JavaScript private (#) vs TypeScript private

AspectTS privateJS #field
EnforcementCompile-time onlyRuntime-enforced
Bracket accessobj['x'] worksobj.#x outside → SyntaxError
InheritanceSubclass still seesTruly private
class Bank {
  #balance = 0;           // JS private, runtime enforced
  private pin: string;    // TS only, compile-time
}

Generics in classes

class Container<T> {
  constructor(private items: T[] = []) {}
  add(item: T) { this.items.push(item); }
  get(i: number): T | undefined { return this.items[i]; }
}

const nums = new Container<number>();
nums.add(42); // ✓
nums.add('x'); // ✗

Rich vs Anemic Domain Model

Anemic (anti-pattern)

class User { id, email, name } - just data holder। Logic UserService.activate(user)-এ procedural disguise।

Rich (Tell Don't Ask)

class User { activate() { if (this.canActivate()) {...} } } - behavior data-এর সাথে। Business invariant enforce।

OOP + FP blend (modern style)

  • Immutable data classes (frozen, readonly properties)
  • Pure method preferable - withX() new instance return (fluent)
  • Side effect gateway-এ (IO, DB) isolate
  • Pattern matching (TS: discriminated union switch) replacing some polymorphism
class Money {
  constructor(readonly amount: number, readonly currency: string) {}
  add(other: Money): Money {
    if (this.currency !== other.currency) throw new Error('mismatch');
    return new Money(this.amount + other.amount, this.currency);
  }
}

Method vs Arrow property - class-এ gotcha

class Counter {
  count = 0;
  method() { this.count++; }          // prototype method, this context-dependent
  arrow = () => { this.count++; };     // instance property, bound this
}

// Handler pass-এ
button.addEventListener('click', counter.method);  // this undefined!
button.addEventListener('click', counter.arrow);   // OK, pre-bound

Abstract method + template pattern

abstract class Report {
  generate(): string {
    return `HEADER\n${this.body()}\nFOOTER`;
  }
  protected abstract body(): string;  // subclass provide
}

class SalesReport extends Report {
  protected body() { return 'sales data...'; }
}

fp.title: Functional Programming
fp.lede: FP = pure functions + immutability + composition। OOP-এর সাথে competition না - complement। Modern codebase-এ OOP (domain modeling) + FP (data transformation, error handling) blend - best of both।
fp.content:
Core principles

PrincipleMeaningExample
Pure functionSame input → same output, no side effectadd(2, 3) - no network/DB/logging
ImmutabilityData never mutate - transform return new[...arr, x] not arr.push(x)
First-class functionsFunction value-এর মতো pass/returnarr.map(fn), compose(f, g)
Higher-order functionsFunction takes/returns functionmap, filter, reduce, curry
Referential transparencyExpression replace-able with value, meaning preserveMemoization-এর foundation
CompositionSmall functions → bigger behaviorpipe(parseNum, validate, save)

Pure vs Impure

// Impure - side effect, external state
let total = 0;
function addToTotal(x) {
  total += x;
  console.log(total);  // side effect
  return total;
}

// Pure - input only, no external mutation
function add(a, b) {
  return a + b;
}

Immutability - JS/TS

// Bad
user.name = 'new';
arr.push(item);
obj.count++;

// FP-style
const updatedUser = { ...user, name: 'new' };
const newArr = [...arr, item];
const newObj = { ...obj, count: obj.count + 1 };

// Immer - mutate-like syntax, immutable behind scenes
const next = produce(state, draft => {
  draft.users[0].name = 'new';
});

Higher-Order Functions - pipeline

const pipeline = [
  (x) => x.trim(),
  (x) => x.toLowerCase(),
  (x) => x.split(' '),
  (x) => x.filter(Boolean),
  (x) => x.join('-')
];

const toSlug = (input) => pipeline.reduce((acc, fn) => fn(acc), input);
toSlug('  Hello World  '); // 'hello-world'

Composition: pipe & compose

const pipe = (...fns) => (x) => fns.reduce((acc, fn) => fn(acc), x);
const compose = (...fns) => (x) => fns.reduceRight((acc, fn) => fn(acc), x);

const addExclaim = pipe(trim, toLower, (s) => s + '!');
addExclaim('  Hello  '); // 'hello!'

Currying & partial application

const curry = (fn) => {
  return function curried(...args) {
    return args.length >= fn.length
      ? fn(...args)
      : (...more) => curried(...args, ...more);
  };
};

const add = curry((a, b, c) => a + b + c);
add(1)(2)(3);      // 6
add(1, 2)(3);       // 6
const add5 = add(5); // partial

Railway-Oriented Programming (ROP)

Error handling-এর FP pattern - Scott Wlaschin (F#) popularized। Value আর error-কে parallel "track"-এ route - conditional try/catch ladder avoid।

// Result type
type Result<T, E = Error> =
  | { ok: true; value: T }
  | { ok: false; error: E };

const ok = <T>(v: T): Result<T> => ({ ok: true, value: v });
const err = <E>(e: E): Result<never, E> => ({ ok: false, error: e });

// Map - success track-এ transform
const map = <T, U, E>(r: Result<T, E>, f: (v: T) => U): Result<U, E> =>
  r.ok ? ok(f(r.value)) : r;

// flatMap (bind) - next step return Result
const flatMap = <T, U, E>(r: Result<T, E>, f: (v: T) => Result<U, E>): Result<U, E> =>
  r.ok ? f(r.value) : r;

// Pipeline - fail fast, no throw
const processUser = (input: string): Result<User, string> => {
  return flatMap(
    flatMap(
      flatMap(parseJson(input), validateShape),
      checkUnique
    ),
    saveToDb
  );
  // any step fail → error track → final Result short-circuits
};

Library: neverthrow, fp-ts, effect-ts (batteries-included ecosystem)। Effect TS 3+ full FP platform with concurrency, retry, dependency injection।

Design by Contract (DbC)

Bertrand Meyer (Eiffel) proposed - function = contract: precondition (caller obligation) + postcondition (callee guarantee) + invariant (class-level always-true)।

function withdraw(account: Account, amount: number): Account {
  // Precondition - caller responsibility
  if (amount <= 0) throw new Error('amount must be positive');
  if (account.balance < amount) throw new Error('insufficient funds');

  const next = { ...account, balance: account.balance - amount };

  // Postcondition - function guarantees
  console.assert(next.balance >= 0, 'balance cannot be negative');
  console.assert(next.balance === account.balance - amount, 'exact amount');

  return next;
  // Invariant (class-level): balance >= 0 always
}

TS + Zod/zod-form-data + custom validator = practical DbC। Runtime contract check + compile-time type + test coverage - defense in depth।

Maybe / Option - null safety

type Maybe<T> = { kind: 'some'; value: T } | { kind: 'none' };

const some = <T>(v: T): Maybe<T> => ({ kind: 'some', value: v });
const none: Maybe<never> = { kind: 'none' };

const findUser = (id: string): Maybe<User> => { ... };

// Chain safely - no null check ladder
const email = pipe(
  findUser('123'),
  mapMaybe(u => u.profile),
  mapMaybe(p => p.email),
  getOrElse('no-email@default.com')
);

Monads simplified

Scary name, simple concept: "container" + map (transform inside) + flatMap (unwrap + re-wrap)।

  • Maybe/Option - value বা nothing
  • Result/Either - success বা error
  • Promise - value-or-pending (technically monad-like)
  • Array - multiple values (flatMap native)
  • IO/Task - lazy side effect description (fp-ts, Effect)

Functional patterns vs OOP patterns

ProblemOOPFP
Algorithm variantsStrategy patternHigher-order function
Add behaviorDecoratorFunction composition
Event notificationObserverStream/Observable
Complex constructionBuilderPartial application / curry
Optional valueNull objectMaybe/Option
State machineState pattern classReducer (pure fn)

Practical JS/TS FP

  • Array methods: map, filter, reduce, flatMap, find, some, every
  • Object: Object.entries/fromEntries + map/filter pipeline
  • Libraries: Ramda, Lodash/fp, fp-ts, Effect, neverthrow, Remeda (TS-first)
  • Reducer pattern: Redux, useReducer - state transition pure function
  • React: components largely pure, useEffect-এ side effect isolate

Functional Core, Imperative Shell

Gary Bernhardt-এর pattern। Business logic (pure functions) core-এ, IO/DB/HTTP thin shell-এ। Core-এর test fast + reliable, shell-এ integration test minimal।

// Functional core - pure
function calculateOrder(cart: Item[], discount: Discount): OrderSummary {
  // pure transformation - no DB, no API
}

// Imperative shell
async function placeOrder(userId: string) {
  const cart = await db.cart.findByUser(userId);         // IO
  const discount = await promo.find(userId);              // IO
  const summary = calculateOrder(cart, discount);         // PURE
  await db.orders.insert(summary);                        // IO
  await queue.publish('order.placed', summary);           // IO
}

When FP over OOP?

  • Data transformation pipeline
  • Concurrent / parallel work (purity = safe share)
  • Error handling (Railway-Oriented)
  • Reducers, state machines

When OOP over FP?

  • Complex domain model with invariant (rich entity, aggregate)
  • Polymorphism-heavy (multi-variant behavior, open extension)
  • Stateful lifecycle (connection, session)
  • Team familiar with OOP language (Java, C#)
Modern advice: Religiously pure FP production-এ rare (Haskell/Elm-এ যারা আছে তাদের বাদে)। Pragmatic blend - rich domain class + FP pipeline + Railway-Oriented error handling = industry standard TS codebase 2026-এ।

solid.title: SOLID Principles
solid.lede: Robert C. Martin (Uncle Bob)-এর 5টা OOP principle। মানলে code maintainable, testable, extensible হয়।
solid.content:
SOLID = 5 principles

LetterPrincipleএক লাইনে
SSingle Responsibilityএকটা class-এর একটাই reason to change
OOpen/ClosedExtension-এ open, modification-এ closed
LLiskov SubstitutionSubclass base class-কে replace করতে পারবে without breaking
IInterface SegregationClient যেন unused method-এর উপর depend না করে
DDependency InversionHigh-level module low-level module-এর উপর না, abstraction-এর উপর depend করবে

S - Single Responsibility

// খারাপ - একই class-এ অনেক কাজ
class User {
  save() { /* DB */ }
  sendEmail() { /* SMTP */ }
  render() { /* UI */ }
}

// ভালো - responsibility আলাদা
class User { ... }
class UserRepository { save(u) {} }
class EmailService { send(u) {} }
class UserView { render(u) {} }

O - Open/Closed

// খারাপ - নতুন shape-এ existing function modify
function area(shape) {
  if (shape.type === 'circle') return ...;
  if (shape.type === 'square') return ...;
}

// ভালো - polymorphism, নতুন shape যোগে existing code অক্ষত
interface Shape { area(): number; }
class Circle implements Shape { area() { ... } }
class Square implements Shape { area() { ... } }

L - Liskov Substitution

Classic violation: Square extends Rectangle। Rectangle-এর setWidth/setHeight independent, Square-এ তা হলে invariant ভাঙে।

I - Interface Segregation

// খারাপ - fat interface
interface Worker { work(); eat(); sleep(); }
class Robot implements Worker { /* eat/sleep অপ্রাসঙ্গিক */ }

// ভালো - segregated
interface Workable { work(); }
interface Eatable { eat(); }

D - Dependency Inversion

// খারাপ - high-level module concrete class-এর উপর
class OrderService {
  private db = new MySQLDatabase(); // ← coupled
}

// ভালো - abstraction-এর উপর
class OrderService {
  constructor(private db: Database) {} // interface
}
new OrderService(new MySQLDatabase());
new OrderService(new PostgresDatabase());
DI (Dependency Injection) = DIP apply করার practical technique। NestJS, Angular-এ built-in; Express-এ manually constructor-এ pass করেন।

Hexagonal Architecture (Ports & Adapters)

           ┌──────────────────────────────┐
           │   Application Core            │
           │   ┌──────────────────┐        │
  HTTP ──→ │───┤ Port (interface) ├──→ Use │
  adapter  │   │  OrderRepo        │    Case│
           │   └──────────────────┘        │
           │                      ↓        │
           └──────────────────────┼────────┘
                                  │
                 ┌────────────────┴───────────────┐
                 │                                │
          Postgres Adapter              In-Memory Adapter (test)

Domain core no framework/DB knowledge - adapter-এ isolate। Test-এ fake adapter, prod-এ real - swap trivial।

Clean Architecture layers

  1. Entities - business rules, framework-independent
  2. Use Cases - application-specific business rules
  3. Interface Adapters - Controllers, Gateways, Presenters
  4. Frameworks & Drivers - Web, DB, UI

Dependency rule: outer → inner only (inner layer outer-কে জানে না)।

DDD tie-in

  • Bounded Context - domain boundary + ubiquitous language
  • Entity - identity-based (User, Order)
  • Value Object - equality by value (Money, Address)
  • Aggregate - consistency boundary, root-এ external access
  • Repository - aggregate load/save
  • Domain Event - significant business happening

SOLID + Testing feedback

Code SOLID-friendly হলে test সহজ - "if hard to test, design smell"।

  • Hard to mock → concrete dependency (DIP violation)
  • Many scenarios → doing too much (SRP)
  • Modify when adding case → OCP violation
  • Mocking fat interface → ISP violation

Anti-patterns

  • God Object - 1000+ line class everything knowing
  • Feature Envy - method অন্য class-এর data বেশি use
  • Shotgun Surgery - small change many files - SRP broken
  • Circular dependency - A ↔ B → extract common C
  • Service Locator - global registry, hidden dependency (DI better)

Practical judgment - when to break rules

Principle guide, dogma না। Small script-এ abstraction over-engineering; large shared codebase-এ rigor pays। Code review checkpoint: "এই abstraction-এর থেকে কে benefit পাচ্ছে, আজ বা ৬ মাস পরে?"

patterns.title: Design Patterns
patterns.lede: Design patterns = common problem-এর reusable solution। Gang of Four (GoF) বই-এ 23টা classic pattern - creational, structural, behavioral তিন বিভাগে।
patterns.content:
তিন category

Categoryকী solve করেExamples
CreationalObject কীভাবে তৈরি হবেSingleton, Factory, Builder, Prototype, Abstract Factory
StructuralObject-রা কীভাবে compose হবেAdapter, Decorator, Facade, Proxy, Composite, Bridge, Flyweight
BehavioralObject-রা কীভাবে communicate করবেObserver, Strategy, Command, Iterator, State, Template, Chain of Responsibility

Singleton

class Config {
  private static instance: Config;
  private constructor() {}
  static getInstance() {
    if (!Config.instance) Config.instance = new Config();
    return Config.instance;
  }
}

ব্যবহার: DB connection, logger, config। সতর্ক: testing কঠিন হয়, global state anti-pattern হতে পারে।

Factory

class ShapeFactory {
  static create(type: string): Shape {
    switch (type) {
      case 'circle': return new Circle();
      case 'square': return new Square();
    }
  }
}

Observer (pub/sub)

class EventEmitter {
  private listeners = new Map<string, Function[]>();
  on(e, cb) { ... }
  emit(e, data) { this.listeners.get(e)?.forEach(cb => cb(data)); }
}

Node.js EventEmitter, DOM event, RxJS - সবই Observer-based।

Strategy

interface PaymentStrategy { pay(amount): void; }
class PayPal implements PaymentStrategy { pay(a) { ... } }
class Stripe implements PaymentStrategy { pay(a) { ... } }

class Checkout {
  constructor(private strategy: PaymentStrategy) {}
  process(amount) { this.strategy.pay(amount); }
}

Decorator

Object-এ dynamically নতুন behavior যোগ করা without subclassing।

function withLogging(fn) {
  return (...args) => {
    console.log('calling', fn.name, args);
    return fn(...args);
  };
}

Adapter

Incompatible interface-কে compatible করা। Third-party library-কে domain-এর সাথে bridge করতে বহুল ব্যবহৃত।

Repository

Data access encapsulate - controller থেকে DB detail hide। Swap MySQL → Postgres easily।

CQRS (Command Query Responsibility Segregation)

Write model (Command) আর read model (Query) আলাদা - different schemas, different DB-ও হতে পারে।

// Write side - normalized
CreateOrderCommand { userId, items }
→ OrderHandler persists to Orders + Lines tables

// Read side - denormalized materialized view
OrderListView { orderId, userName, total, itemNames }
→ eventual consistency via projection

Complex domain, read-heavy/write-heavy skew, different scaling needs-এ ব্যবহার। Overkill simple CRUD-এ।

Event Sourcing

// Instead of storing current state:
// UPDATE account SET balance = 500

// Store events:
// INSERT INTO events: (id=1, type='AccountOpened', amount=100)
// INSERT INTO events: (id=2, type='Deposited', amount=500)
// INSERT INTO events: (id=3, type='Withdrew', amount=100)

// Current state = replay all events
// balance = 100 + 500 - 100 = 500
  • Benefits: audit trail free, rebuild projection, time-travel, bug fix retroactive
  • Trade-offs: complexity, query latency, schema evolution tricky
  • Pair with: CQRS (event store = write, projection = read)

Saga pattern - distributed transaction replacement

Microservice-এ 2PC impractical। Saga = sequence of local transaction, fail হলে compensating transaction।

Choreography vs Orchestration

StyleHowProsCons
ChoreographyService event listen + respondLoose coupling, no SPOFHard to debug, workflow implicit
OrchestrationCentral coordinator (Temporal, Camunda)Visible workflow, easier debugOrchestrator bottleneck, tight coupling

Unit of Work

const uow = new UnitOfWork(db);
const user = uow.users.create({...});
const order = uow.orders.create({...});
uow.orders.addLine(order.id, {...});
await uow.commit();  // single DB transaction

ORM-এ built-in - Entity Framework DbContext, Hibernate Session, Doctrine EntityManager।

Specification pattern

class ActiveUsersSpec {
  isSatisfiedBy(user) { return user.active && !user.banned; }
  and(other) { ... }
  or(other) { ... }
}

repo.findAll(new ActiveUsersSpec().and(new PremiumUsersSpec()));

Complex query encapsulate + compose। Over-kill simple cases, domain-rich query-তে shine।

Anti-patterns - do not repeat

  • Singleton abuse - global state, testing nightmare। Use DI
  • God class - 1000+ lines, violates SRP loudly
  • Premature abstraction - 1 implementation-এর interface, speculative
  • Mock-the-world test - all layers mocked, integration miss
  • Manager/Helper naming - doesn't say what it does

Pattern selection cheat sheet

ProblemPattern
Runtime algorithm swapStrategy
Object build step-by-stepBuilder
Subsystem has complex APIFacade
Need to wrap + add behaviorDecorator
Tree structure traverseComposite + Visitor
Notify multiple listenerObserver / pub-sub
Request queue / undoCommand
Legacy API incompatibleAdapter

database.title: Database Queries
database.lede: Slow query থেকে outage - prod-এ সবচেয়ে common bug। Index, query plan, N+1, join - এগুলো বুঝলে 90% issue solve।
database.content:
Index কী ও কেন

Index = phone book-এর মতো sorted structure (সাধারণত B-tree)। WHERE/ORDER BY/JOIN-এ lookup O(log n)।

-- একটা common query
SELECT * FROM posts WHERE user_id = 42;

-- index না থাকলে full scan (slow)
CREATE INDEX idx_posts_user ON posts(user_id);

Composite index - leftmost prefix

CREATE INDEX idx ON orders(user_id, status, created_at);

-- ব্যবহার হবে:
WHERE user_id = 1                         -- ✓
WHERE user_id = 1 AND status = 'paid'     -- ✓
WHERE user_id = 1 AND status = 'paid' AND created_at > ... -- ✓

-- হবে না (leftmost missing):
WHERE status = 'paid'                     -- ✗

Query plan - EXPLAIN

EXPLAIN ANALYZE
SELECT * FROM posts WHERE user_id = 42;

দেখবেন: Seq Scan (bad) vs Index Scan (good), estimated rows, actual rows, cost।

N+1 problem

// N+1 (খারাপ)
const users = await User.findAll();          // 1 query
for (const u of users) {
  u.posts = await Post.findBy({ user: u });  // N queries
}

// Fix: eager load
const users = await User.findAll({
  include: [Post]
});  // 1 query with JOIN, or 2 with IN

JOIN types

TypeReturns
INNER JOINদুই table-এ match থাকলে
LEFT JOINLeft-এর সব, right-এ না থাকলে NULL
RIGHT JOINRight-এর সব
FULL OUTER JOINদুই দিকের সব
CROSS JOINCartesian product

ACID

  • Atomicity - all-or-nothing
  • Consistency - valid state to valid state
  • Isolation - concurrent transactions দেখে না
  • Durability - commit-এর পর survive crash

Transaction isolation levels

LevelPrevents
Read Uncommittedকিছু না (dirty read possible)
Read CommittedDirty read
Repeatable ReadDirty + non-repeatable read
Serializableসব (phantom read সহ)

Normalization summary

  • 1NF - atomic column, no array
  • 2NF - 1NF + no partial dependency on composite key
  • 3NF - 2NF + no transitive dependency

Replication patterns

PatternকীভাবেTrade-off
Primary → Replica (async)Write primary-এ, read replica থেকে। WAL async ship করে।Read scale। Replica lag - stale read।
Primary → Replica (sync)Commit-এর আগে replica-ও confirm করেNo data loss। Latency ↑।
Multi-primaryসব node write acceptActive-active। Conflict resolution লাগে (last-write-wins, CRDT)।
Logical replicationRow-level change streamSelective table/column replicate। Version upgrade, ETL।

Scaling strategies

  1. Vertical scale - bigger box (CPU/RAM/disk)। Simple কিন্তু limit আছে।
  2. Read replica - read traffic offload। 80% workload read হলে huge win।
  3. Caching layer - Redis/Memcached in front। Hot data hit rate বাড়ায়।
  4. Connection pooling - PgBouncer/ProxySQL। Too many client connection problem solve।
  5. Sharding - horizontal partition shard key দিয়ে। Ultimate scale কিন্তু cross-shard query painful।
  6. Read-only archive - cold data cheap storage-এ (S3/Glacier) move।

Consistency models

ModelGuarantee
StrongRead সবসময় latest write দেখে (linearizable)
Eventualকিছু সময় পর converge - DynamoDB default, CF KV
Read-your-writesনিজের write immediately দেখেন, others-এর না
Monotonic readRead time-এ backward যাবে না

CAP theorem

Network partition (P) occur করলে Consistency (C) বা Availability (A) - যেকোনো একটা sacrifice করতে হবে।

  • CP - Postgres primary (partition হলে unavailable হতে পারে, কিন্তু consistent)
  • AP - DynamoDB, Cassandra (সবসময় available, eventual consistency)
  • CA - single-node only (network partition ছাড়া)

dbtypes.title: Database Types
dbtypes.lede: এক size-এ সব fit না - workload অনুযায়ী right DB। SQL, document, KV, graph, columnar, time-series, vector - প্রতিটার strong point আলাদা।
dbtypes.content:
Main categories

TypeExamplesBest for
Relational (SQL)Postgres, MySQL, SQLite, MariaDBTransactions, joins, complex queries, reporting
DocumentMongoDB, CouchDB, FirestoreNested JSON, flexible schema, rapid evolution
Key-ValueRedis, DynamoDB (KV mode), Cloudflare KV, Deno KV, ValkeyCache, session, simple lookup, super low-latency
Wide-columnCassandra, ScyllaDB, BigTableMassive write throughput, time-series at scale
GraphNeo4j, Neptune, ArangoDBSocial graph, recommendations, deeply connected data
Time-seriesInfluxDB, TimescaleDB, PrometheusMetrics, IoT, append-heavy sorted-by-time
Vectorpgvector, Pinecone, Weaviate, Qdrant, MilvusSemantic search, RAG, AI embeddings
SearchElasticsearch, Meilisearch, TypesenseFull-text, fuzzy, faceted search

Key-Value store comparison

StoreLatencyPersistenceবিশেষত্ব
Redis< 1msIn-memory + RDB/AOFRich data structures (list, set, sorted set, hash, stream, pub/sub), scripting, modules
Valkey< 1msSame as RedisRedis fork (Linux Foundation), BSD-licensed, drop-in replacement
Memcached< 1msIn-memory onlySimple string KV, multi-threaded, smaller feature set
DynamoDB5-10msManaged SSDAWS managed, serverless, KV + document, GSI/LSI
Cloudflare KV< 50ms read globally (cached)Eventually consistent, global edgeWorker-integrated, ~60s write propagation, read-heavy
Deno KVFoundationDB under hoodStrong consistencyNative Deno API, ACID transactions, globally replicated
Cloudflare Durable Objectsms-levelStrongly consistent per-objectStateful actors at edge, perfect for realtime/collaborative state

KV vs Document - কখন কোনটা

Key-Value

"Key দিয়ে value fetch" - simple। Complex query নেই। Cache, session, feature flag।

Document

Nested JSON, secondary index, partial update, query by field। Product catalog, user profile, CMS।

Relational

Multi-table relation, ACID transaction, ad-hoc reporting query।

Serverless DB (2026)

DBBase engineবিশেষত্ব
NeonPostgresBranchable (like git), scale-to-zero, fast cold start
PlanetScaleMySQL / Postgres (Vitess)Schema branching, no downtime migration, global read replica
TursolibSQL (SQLite fork)Edge-replicated SQLite, embedded option, tiny per-DB cost
SupabasePostgresAuth + Storage + Realtime + Edge Functions bundled
Cloudflare D1SQLiteWorkers-integrated, global replicas, HTTP-native
Aurora Serverless v2MySQL / PostgresAWS managed, auto-scaling ACU, production-grade
XataPostgres + searchHybrid DB + search, SDK-first
2026 trend: Serverless Postgres (Neon, Supabase) + edge SQLite (Turso, D1) dominant। Branch-per-PR workflow standard হয়ে যাচ্ছে।

Vector DB workflow (RAG)

User query → embedding model → vector
                                  ↓
            Vector DB similarity search (top-K)
                                  ↓
            Retrieved context + prompt → LLM

Postgres-এ pgvector extension-ই বেশিরভাগ use case-এ যথেষ্ট - আলাদা vector DB না থাকলেও চলে।

Vector DB internals - HNSW index

Hierarchical Navigable Small World - multi-layer graph, top-layer sparse jump, bottom-layer dense neighbors। Approximate NN search O(log n)। Recall vs speed trade-off - M (neighbors), ef (search width) tune করুন।

  • Distance metrics: cosine similarity (direction), L2 (magnitude), dot-product
  • Dimension reduction: embedding 1536 → 256 (PCA/MRL) - smaller/faster, some quality loss
  • Hybrid search: vector + keyword (BM25) combine for best relevance

Time-series DB specifics

  • Hypertable / chunk: time range-এ partition, old chunk compress/drop
  • Continuous aggregates: 1-min data → 1-hour pre-aggregated, query fast
  • Retention policy: > 30 days raw drop, hourly aggregate keep 1 year
  • Examples: TimescaleDB (Postgres ext), InfluxDB (purpose-built), Prometheus (metric-specific), QuestDB (SQL + column-store)

Graph DB traversal

// Neo4j Cypher
MATCH (u:User {id: 1})-[:FRIEND*1..3]-(friend)
WHERE NOT (u)-[:FRIEND]-(friend)
RETURN friend LIMIT 10;
// Friends of friends, up to 3 hops

SQL-এ recursive CTE দিয়েও possible কিন্তু graph DB native-এ fast। Social graph, fraud detection, knowledge graph use-case।

Columnar (OLAP) vs Row-store (OLTP)

AspectRow-storeColumn-store
StorageRow-wiseColumn-wise
Insert/updateFastSlow
Scan full columnReads allOnly that column
CompressionModerateExcellent (similar data adjacent)
Use caseTransactions (Postgres, MySQL)Analytics (BigQuery, Redshift, ClickHouse, DuckDB)

Modern hybrid engines

  • TiDB - MySQL-compatible, HTAP (OLTP + OLAP one engine)
  • CockroachDB - distributed Postgres, strong consistency
  • YugabyteDB - multi-region, Postgres/Cassandra APIs
  • SingleStore - row + column hybrid, in-memory
  • DuckDB - embedded OLAP (SQLite-এর analytics version), Pandas replacement

Specialized modern DBs (2026)

General-purpose Postgres সব use case cover করে না। একটা নতুন category - purpose-built DB - নির্দিষ্ট workload-এ 100-1000x gain করে।

TigerBeetle - financial/OLTP transactions

// Two entities only: Accounts + Transfers
client.createAccounts([{ id: 1, code: 718, ledger: 1 }]);
client.createTransfers([{
  id: 1,
  debit_account_id: 1,
  credit_account_id: 2,
  amount: 100,
  ledger: 1
}]);
  • Domain: double-entry accounting, financial ledger - payments, wallets, accounting, billing
  • Performance: 1000x throughput vs Postgres (specialized opcodes)
  • Architecture: Zig-written, statically allocated, deterministic, VSR consensus (like Paxos/Raft)
  • Guarantees: strict serializability, real-time balance limit, automated failover
  • Trade-off: only double-entry schema - general DB-এর বদলে নয়, সঙ্গে use - data plane (hot path)-এ TigerBeetle, control plane-এ Postgres
  • Use: Stripe-scale payment, exchange, wallet। Financial integrity critical

Convex - reactive TypeScript backend

// convex/messages.ts
import { query, mutation } from './_generated/server';

export const list = query(async (ctx) => {
  return await ctx.db.query('messages').collect();
});

export const send = mutation(async (ctx, args: { body: string }) => {
  await ctx.db.insert('messages', { body: args.body });
});

// React client - auto-reactive
const messages = useQuery(api.messages.list);
// DB change → query auto re-run → UI update
  • Domain: reactive full-stack app, TypeScript everywhere
  • Model: Postgres-like + real-time subscriptions। Query = TS function, auto-track dependency, data change → client push
  • Features: DB + file storage + scheduling + actions + vector search + auth - unified TS platform
  • ACID: serializable transactions, optimistic concurrency
  • Open-source: self-host possible, Convex Cloud managed option
  • Use: collaborative app, live dashboard, realtime UI without WebSocket manual code

SpacetimeDB - realtime multiplayer/game DB

// Rust module (compiled to WASM, runs inside DB)
#[spacetimedb(table(public))]
pub struct Player { id: u64, name: String, x: f32, y: f32 }

#[spacetimedb(reducer)]
pub fn move_player(ctx: ReducerContext, x: f32, y: f32) {
  // direct table access, clients auto-sync
}

// Client (Rust/C#/TS/C++) subscribes, state auto-streamed
  • Domain: MMO games, realtime multiplayer, shared 3D world
  • Model: relational DB + server - no middle tier - clients connect directly to DB
  • Language: server logic (reducer) Rust/TS/C#/C++। Client SDK same languages
  • Performance: Rust-written, in-memory-first, SpacetimeDB 2.0 (Feb 2026) significant gains
  • Use: MMO (Bitcraft-scale), realtime collaborative sim, anything requiring authoritative shared state

Specialized DB comparison

DBPrimary workloadAbstraction levelUse when
TigerBeetleFinancial ledgerTwo built-in entities onlyPayment, wallet, double-entry - data plane
ConvexReactive TS appFunctions + tables + real-time subsFull-stack TS, live UI, ACID OK
SpacetimeDBRealtime multiplayerServer logic in DB (reducers)MMO, game, high-frequency shared state
SupabasePostgres + Auth + RealtimeDB + SDK + Edge FunctionsTraditional CRUD + real-time, open-source
FirebaseMobile reactiveFirestore/RTDB + Auth + FunctionsMobile-first, Google ecosystem
PlanetScale/NeonServerless Postgres/MySQLBranchable schemaPer-PR preview, scale-to-zero

Reactive DBs - architectural shift

Traditional: client polls/subscribes manually, server maintains cache invalidation। Reactive DB (Convex, SpacetimeDB) এই pattern invert করে - query = live view, DB push change। WebSocket/SSE manual plumbing eliminate। Trade-off: vendor lock-in, scale cost, not SQL-standard।

Pick the right DB - decision tree

  1. Transactions + relations + reporting? → Postgres (safe default)
  2. Flexible schema, document model? → MongoDB বা Postgres JSONB
  3. Cache, session, rate limit? → Redis
  4. Massive writes, time-series? → Cassandra/ScyllaDB or TimescaleDB
  5. Global edge read-heavy? → Cloudflare KV/D1, Turso
  6. AI embeddings? → pgvector first, scale হলে Pinecone/Qdrant
  7. Full-text + relevance? → Elasticsearch/Meilisearch or Postgres tsvector
  8. Social network? → Neo4j or Postgres recursive CTE
  9. Payment/ledger/financial? → TigerBeetle (data plane) + Postgres (control plane)
  10. Realtime reactive TS app? → Convex
  11. Multiplayer/game/shared world? → SpacetimeDB

queues.title: Job Queues & Brokers
queues.lede: "User signup-এর সাথে-সাথে email পাঠাতে হবে" - synchronous হলে signup slow, email server down হলে signup fail। Job queue decouple করে - signup immediate respond, email task queue-এ পড়ে, worker process করে।
queues.content:
কেন queue দরকার?

  • Decoupling - producer আর consumer আলাদা service হতে পারে।
  • Retry + reliability - worker fail করলে job পরে আবার try।
  • Smoothing / backpressure - burst traffic-এ DB/external API overwhelm হয় না।
  • Scheduling - delayed/cron-based job।
  • Scaling - worker horizontally বাড়ানো যায়।

তিন pattern

PatternDescriptionTool
Task QueueProducer → FIFO queue → 1 worker consumeBullMQ, Celery, Sidekiq, SQS
Pub/SubProducer → topic → multiple subscribers সবাই copy পায়Redis Pub/Sub, SNS, Google Pub/Sub
Event Stream / LogImmutable ordered log, consumers নিজের offset track করে, replay possibleKafka, AWS Kinesis, Redpanda

Popular tools

ToolBroker/Runtimeবিশেষত্ব
BullMQRedisNode.js, priority + rate-limit + cron, modern (Bull v5)
CeleryRabbitMQ / RedisPython ecosystem default, huge plugin set
RabbitMQAMQP 0.9.1 protocolExchange + queue routing, mature enterprise
ActiveMQ / ArtemisJMS, AMQP, STOMPJava world, long history
Apache KafkaDistributed logHuge throughput, event sourcing, stream processing
AWS SQSManaged queueStandard (at-least-once) + FIFO (exactly-once)
SidekiqRedisRuby/Rails standard
Temporal / InngestDurable executionWorkflow + retry state DB-এ

Delivery guarantees

  • At-most-once - message lost হতে পারে, duplicate নেই। Fast, lossy।
  • At-least-once - duplicate হতে পারে, lost হবে না। Most common; consumer idempotent লাগবে।
  • Exactly-once - hardest। Kafka transactions, SQS FIFO + dedup।

Idempotency

At-least-once-এ একই message দুইবার আসতে পারে। Consumer-এ idempotency key check:

async function handleJob({ orderId }) {
  if (await processed.has(orderId)) return;
  await charge(orderId);
  await processed.add(orderId);
}

Retry + exponential backoff + DLQ

// BullMQ example
queue.add('email', data, {
  attempts: 5,
  backoff: { type: 'exponential', delay: 1000 }
  // 1s, 2s, 4s, 8s, 16s
});

5 attempt-এ fail → Dead Letter Queue-এ move, manual inspection / alert।

Kafka specifics

  • Topic - named stream।
  • Partition - topic-কে parallelism-এর জন্য split; ordering per-partition।
  • Consumer group - একই group-এর consumer-রা partitions ভাগ করে; different group independently।
  • Offset - consumer নিজের progress track করে।

RabbitMQ exchange types

ExchangeRouting
DirectExact routing key match
TopicPattern (order.*, payment.#)
Fanoutসব bound queue-এ copy
HeadersHeader attribute match
Decision tips:
  • Node app + Redis already আছে → BullMQ
  • Python app → Celery
  • Complex routing / multi-language → RabbitMQ
  • Event sourcing / high throughput analytics → Kafka
  • AWS lock-in OK → SQS
  • Stateful workflow / sagas → Temporal / Inngest

Outbox pattern - critical for reliability

Problem: DB write + message publish - atomicity কীভাবে? Distributed transaction expensive/unavailable।

// Same transaction:
BEGIN;
  INSERT INTO orders (id, ...) VALUES (...);
  INSERT INTO outbox (event_type, payload) VALUES ('OrderCreated', ...);
COMMIT;

// Background relay:
// Poll outbox → publish to queue → mark processed
// OR use Postgres logical replication + Debezium
  • DB commit = event guaranteed
  • Relay service eventual consistency
  • Idempotent consumer (duplicate possible on relay crash)

Inbox pattern - consumer-side dedup

// On receive:
BEGIN;
  IF NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM inbox WHERE msg_id = $id) THEN
    INSERT INTO inbox (msg_id, received_at) VALUES (...);
    -- process the message logic
  END IF;
COMMIT;

At-least-once-এ duplicate arrive করলে second time no-op। Outbox + Inbox = end-to-end exactly-once effective।

Saga - long-running workflow

Choreography example (event-driven)

OrderService.create()
  → publishes OrderCreated
  → PaymentService consumes, charges, publishes PaymentSuccess
  → InventoryService consumes, reserves, publishes InventoryReserved
  → OrderService consumes, marks ready

Orchestration example (Temporal)

@Workflow
async function orderWorkflow(order) {
  await activities.charge(order);
  try {
    await activities.reserveInventory(order);
  } catch (e) {
    await activities.refund(order);  // compensation
    throw e;
  }
  await activities.shipOrder(order);
}

Priority lanes strategy

// Separate queues, dedicated worker pools
HighPriorityQueue  (workers: 10)  - payment, auth
MediumPriorityQueue (workers: 20) - email, notification
LowPriorityQueue    (workers: 5)  - analytics, report

// BullMQ - same queue, priority score
queue.add('job', data, { priority: 1 });  // 1 = highest

Single priority queue-এ low-priority flood-এ high starve হতে পারে - dedicated pool safer।

Fan-out / Fan-in pattern

// Fan-out
parent.spawn([child1, child2, child3]);

// Wait for all (fan-in)
await Promise.all(children);
parent.complete();

Image processing - parent enqueue thumbnail + preview + OCR parallel → all done-এ report। BullMQ Flow, Temporal child workflow।

Scheduling + cron

ToolDurabilityMissed execution
OS cronNoLost
BullMQ repeatRedis-basedCatch-up on restart (configurable)
EventBridge SchedulerAWS managedAt-least-once
Temporal CronFull durableGuaranteed

Monitoring - key metrics

  • Queue depth - backlog indicator
  • Processing time p50/p99 - worker capacity
  • Error rate - retries, DLQ velocity
  • Lag / age of oldest message - SLO critical
  • Consumer health - heartbeat, lag per consumer group (Kafka)

Common operational pitfalls

  • Worker crash-এ message re-deliver - idempotency mandatory
  • Retry storm → circuit breaker + exponential backoff + jitter
  • Poison message loops - max retry + DLQ
  • Shared DB connection pool exhaust - worker concurrency tune
  • Kafka partition count fixed - scale planning
  • DLQ ignored → silent data loss, alert + ownership

frameworks.title: Backend Frameworks 2026
frameworks.lede: Express 10+ বছর ecosystem king, কিন্তু 2026-এ আরো দ্রুত, type-safe alternatives available। Hono, Elysia, Encore.ts, Fastify, NestJS - প্রতিটার sweet spot আলাদা।
frameworks.content:
Framework landscape - 2026 benchmark

Benchmark claims framework authors-এর own - interpret carefully। Real-world (DB, middleware) performance gap দুই framework-এর মধ্যে অনেক কমে যায়।

FrameworkClaimed perf (Express = 1x baseline)Primary runtimeSweet spot
Elysia~10x+ (294K req/s plain, 224K w/validation - Bun 1.3.2, Nov 2025)BunBun-native, Exact Mirror JIT, bun compile optimization
Encore.ts~5-7x (139K req/s plain, 96K w/validation - same hardware)Node (Rust runtime under hood)Infrastructure + observability built-in
Hono~3xAny (Web Standards)Edge deployment, portability
Fastify~2-3xNodeSchema-based validation, plugin system
NestJS~1-1.5xNodeEnterprise, OOP + DI + decorators
Express1xNodeBiggest ecosystem, legacy, simplicity
Bun.serve (raw)~10x+BunNo framework overhead, max throughput
Benchmark war note (2026): Encore claimed 3x over Elysia earlier in 2025। Elysia-এর Nov 2025 re-test (same hardware, i7-13700K, Bun 1.3.2, bun compile build) দেখায় Elysia actually 2x Encore - with validation Elysia 224K vs Encore 96K req/s। Numbers methodology-sensitive - real app measure নিজে করুন।

Hono - edge-first portable

import { Hono } from 'hono';
const app = new Hono();

app.get('/', (c) => c.text('Hello'));
app.get('/users/:id', (c) => c.json({ id: c.req.param('id') }));

export default app;
// Deploy as-is: Cloudflare Workers, Deno, Bun, Node, Netlify, Supabase
  • Web Standards API (Request, Response) core - zero runtime lock-in
  • Tiny footprint (~30KB) - fast cold start
  • Built-in JWT, cors, logger, basic-auth middleware
  • RPC mode (client SDK auto-gen like tRPC)
  • OpenAPI integration - @hono/zod-openapi

Elysia - Bun-native, TS-first

import { Elysia, t } from 'elysia';

const app = new Elysia()
  .post('/user', ({ body }) => ({ id: 1, ...body }), {
    body: t.Object({
      email: t.String({ format: 'email' }),
      age: t.Number({ minimum: 18 })
    })
  })
  .listen(3000);

export type App = typeof app; // client type import
  • Bun-exclusive (Node/Deno-তে চলবে না)
  • Static code analysis - route compile-time optimize
  • TypeBox schema - validation + TS type single source
  • End-to-end type safety (Eden client)
  • Plugins: JWT, cors, swagger, websocket

Encore.ts - infrastructure framework

// Define API + infra as code
import { api, APIError } from 'encore.dev/api';
import { SQLDatabase } from 'encore.dev/storage/sqldb';
import { Subscription, Topic } from 'encore.dev/pubsub';

const db = new SQLDatabase('users');
const userEvents = new Topic('user.events');

export const createUser = api(
  { method: 'POST', path: '/users', expose: true },
  async ({ email }: { email: string }) => {
    const id = await db.queryRow`INSERT INTO users(email) ...`;
    await userEvents.publish({ userId: id });
    return { id };
  }
);
  • API + DB + Pub/Sub + cron + tracing - single framework
  • Deploy to your AWS/GCP (not Encore Cloud lock-in)
  • Auto distributed tracing, metric, log
  • Runtime type safety (Rust-based validation engine)
  • Auto-generated dashboard, API docs

Fastify - Express-in-Node, faster

import Fastify from 'fastify';
const app = Fastify({ logger: true });

app.get('/users/:id', {
  schema: {
    params: { type: 'object', properties: { id: { type: 'string' } } },
    response: { 200: { type: 'object', properties: { id: { type: 'string' } } } }
  }
}, async (req) => ({ id: req.params.id }));

await app.listen({ port: 3000 });
  • JSON Schema → fast serialize/deserialize (Ajv-based)
  • Plugin encapsulation (scoped, like NestJS module)
  • Mature, stable, production-proven
  • Express migration path relatively simple

NestJS - OOP enterprise

@Controller('users')
export class UserController {
  constructor(private readonly userService: UserService) {}

  @Post()
  async create(@Body() dto: CreateUserDto) {
    return this.userService.create(dto);
  }
}
  • Angular-inspired: decorators, DI container, modules
  • Built-in: TypeORM, Mongoose, GraphQL, WebSocket, microservices
  • Testability via DI - mock-friendly
  • Heavy learning curve, some boilerplate overhead
  • Large team / enterprise fit; overkill for simple API

tRPC - not a framework, full-stack RPC

  • TS-first, no codegen - server types directly consumed by client
  • Monorepo-friendly - Next.js/TanStack Start integration
  • Built-in validation (Zod), middleware, subscriptions
  • API contract = TS types - source of truth
  • Alternative to REST/OpenAPI when team owns full stack

Decision matrix

NeedPick
Maximum throughput, Bun OKElysia + Bun
Edge deploy, global latencyHono (Cloudflare Workers)
Infra + observability includedEncore.ts
Node refugee from Express, speed bumpFastify
Enterprise, team familiar with Java/Spring/AngularNestJS
Largest npm plugin need, legacyExpress
Same-team full-stack TS monorepotRPC (+ any HTTP framework)
Zero framework, max perf, BunBun.serve raw
Hono's big advantage: same codebase runs on Cloudflare Workers, Deno Deploy, Netlify Edge, Bun, Node, AWS Lambda। Lock-in কমে - runtime change হলেও code untouched।

Migration path - Express → modern

  1. Minimal change: Express 4 → Express 5 (async support)
  2. Moderate: Express → Fastify (similar middleware model, schema addition)
  3. Bigger: Express → Hono (Web Standards rewrite)
  4. Full-stack: Express → tRPC (if monorepo + same team + TS everywhere)
  5. Enterprise: Express → NestJS (OOP + DI restructure)

runtimes.title: Runtimes & Deployment
runtimes.lede: JavaScript এক ভাষা, কিন্তু 2026-এ runtime আর deployment target অনেক। Node.js, Bun, Deno - তিন major runtime। Cloudflare Workers, Deno Deploy, Netlify Edge, Supabase Edge, Bun-on-edge - deployment targets। প্রতিটার trade-off আলাদা।
runtimes.content:
তিন major runtime - quick compare

AspectNode.jsBunDeno
JS engineV8JavaScriptCore (WebKit)V8
LanguageC++ZigRust
HTTP throughput (2026 benchmark)~13K req/s~52K req/s (2.7x Node)~22K req/s
Startup time60-120ms8-15ms40-60ms
Package install (medium project)npm: 20sbun: 1sdeno: 17s
TypeScriptExperimental (--experimental-strip-types)Native, zero-configNative, zero-config
SecurityPermission model (opt-in)LimitedSecure-by-default - explicit flags
Built-in toolingMinimal (test runner in 18+)Bundler, test, install, runnerTest, lint, fmt, bundle, task, REPL
Module systemCJS + ESMCJS + ESM + TS autoESM + URL imports + npm:
EcosystemLargest (npm, 2M+ packages)npm-compatiblejsr + npm (npm: prefix)

Why different engines matter

  • JavaScriptCore (Bun) - fast startup, low memory, optimized for short-lived scripts। Lambda/edge-friendly।
  • V8 (Node, Deno, Chrome) - long-running optimization, JIT heuristics mature। Long-running server-এ excellent।

Bun's standout features

  • Built-in bundler (bun build) - esbuild-এর replacement
  • Native Postgres (Bun.sql), Redis (Bun.redis), SQLite (bun:sqlite) drivers
  • Bun.serve() - native HTTP with routes + WebSocket + HTTPS
  • Bun.Shell (Bun.$`ls`) - execa replacement
  • Auto-loads .env (no dotenv)
  • HTML imports - React + Tailwind bundling out-of-box
  • Node compatibility - most npm packages work

Deno's standout features

  • Secure by default - no file/net/env access without flag
  • URL imports - import x from 'https://deno.land/...'
  • JSR (JavaScript Registry) - alternative to npm, first-class TS
  • Web-standards first - fetch, Request, Response, URL
  • Built-in deploy to Deno Deploy (edge globally)
  • Deno KV - FoundationDB-backed native DB
  • Deno 2 (2024) added Node compat, npm: prefix - migration path easier

Deployment targets - 2026 landscape

TargetRuntimeCold startBest for
Cloudflare WorkersV8 isolate (not Node)< 1msGlobal low-latency, 330+ cities, massive scale
Deno DeployV8 isolate + Deno stdlib< 50msNode.js refugee, TS native, 30+ regions
Netlify Edge FunctionsDeno runtime (via Deno Deploy infra)< 50msJamstack sites, middleware, edge rendering
Supabase Edge FunctionsDeno (self-hosted)~50msSupabase stack-tight (auth/DB/storage access), open-source, PostgreSQL-native
Fastly ComputeWebAssembly< 50μsMulti-language, portable
AWS Lambda@EdgeNode container100-1000msCloudFront integration
Bun on Railway/FlyBun native10-50msSmall API, db-driven, low cost
Kubernetes (EKS/GKE)Any (Node/Bun/Deno)Depends on podEnterprise, heavy workload

Edge runtime limitations - important

Cloudflare Workers, Netlify Edge, Deno Deploy - V8/Deno isolate based। Full Node API নেই:

  • No fs, no child_process, no native addons
  • Memory limit typically 128MB
  • CPU time limit (Workers free: 10ms, paid: 30s)
  • Heavy deps (sharp, puppeteer, ffmpeg) - won't work
  • Web Standards API only - fetch, Request, Response, Streams, crypto.subtle

Hybrid architecture - 2026 pattern

User
  │
  ▼
┌──────────────────────────┐
